calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee And Establishing Procedure For The Appointment Of Future Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Jan-09-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kimberly Duggan appeared by zoom for Felix Wai (petitioner).
Kevin Rodriguez appeared by zoom for and with Mark Unger (temporary trustee/proposed trustee) by zoom.
Dan Spector appeared by zoom for Grace Wissman, Quanna Jew and Robert Wai (objectors).
Margaret Wai (niece/remainder beneficiary) appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Shayna Montgomery (reporter) appeared by zoom.

The court noted that it read the extensive document that was filed by Mrs. Duggan, that there is a request for more time, that only petition before it is to have a successor trustee appointed, and that there is no competing petition. Discussion was held regarding the issue of whether or not it is appropriate for the court to retain jurisdiction and bring the trust under court supervision, the parties being unsuccessful in obtaining a consent form from a professional fiduciary, the need of having a non-temporary trustee in place to address the complex issues that require attention, the issue of Felix having perjured himself to the court, whether or not it is appropriate for the court to continue the matter and order Mr. Unger to file a report, etc. The Petition for Order Appointing Successor Trustee, etc. is under submission with the intent to grant. The court will decline the request to order a report by Mr. Unger.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Reporter: Shayna Montgomery, CSR #13452, 602-501-5814, shaynamontgomery26@gmail.com

Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee And Establishing Procedure For The Appointment Of Future Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Dec-11-2023 Continued To Jan-08-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee And Establishing Procedure For The Appointment Of Future Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Oct-25-2023 Continued To Dec-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Kimberly Duggan, Esq. for Felix Wai, Petitioner. Daniel Spector, Esq. for Robert Wai; Quana Jew, attorney-in-fact, for Miranda Jew; Grace Wai Wissman, individually and as attorney-in-fact for Yuk Ying Wai, Objectors. Kevin Rodriquez, Esq. for Mark Under, Temporary Trustee.
Jacqueline Kwan, Interested Party.

The Court stated that it had read the recently filed objections. Ms. Duggan noted that she just reviewed the objections. Mr. Spector requested that the Court continue the matter 60 days to allow time to obtain docments/continue talking with fiduciaries, that by then they would have the documents/consents/declinations, that they would have exhausted their resources, and that during this time things should be limited to/focused on maintaining the status quo such as paying caregivers and taxes. Ms. Duggan stated that she contacted Mr. Spector on Monday for a continuance and he declined, that Mr. Spector can bring a separate petition, that no corporate trustee is interested, that there are no liquid assets, and that she is asking the Court to appoint Mr. Unger today. Argument/discussions are held regarding the Court issuing an order limiting Mr. Unger to not do anything transformative during this time period. Ms. Duggan argued that there is $75,000,000.00 in debt, that assets do need to be sold, that $2,500,000.00 in taxes is approaching, and that there needs to be sufficient liquidity to cover the taxes, etc. Ms. Duggan further argued for Mr. Unger to set up and share information with the remainder contingent beneficiaries, stated that there are over 100 properties in this portfolio, that the portfolio should be diversified for all beneficiaries, that they were trying to obtain a corporate fiduciary, and that they contacted one who declined. Mr. Spector stated that there was a property that was sold, that the deed was signed by Ms. Duggan's client, and that they need to know where those funds went so they can be used for taxes, caregivers, etc. Mr. Rodriguez stated that there were sales that were going to happen in 60 days, that those funds would be used to pay ongoing costs, that they are not trying to act "behind the curtain", that this is not the best situation for a 90-year old man, and that he would request that the Court decline to "pause" this for 60 days. The Court stated that it was expecting counsel for Mr. Unger to say that they were not taking any big actions and that things would remain status quo for 60 days. The Court inquired about John Wai's well-being. Mr. Spector stated that this was the first that he was hearing about any transactions that need to close in 60 days and that there should be a supplement for the parties/Court. Mr. Rodriguez stated that they have not received any requests to discuss these matters, that his client has reviewed the finances and had a meeting with the remainder contingent beneficiaries so they could ask any questions, that there will be a harm if the Court limits his client's power, that his client needs to generate liquidity, that there are significant properties in the portfolio that do well, and that his client is a bonded private fiduciary. Ms. Duggan noted that her client has durable power of attorney for John Wai and has On Lok PACE in place, etc. Mr. Spector stated that they interviewed BNY Mellon and will contact one more. The Court ordered Mr. Rodriguez and Mr. Unger to disclose generally to counsel and their clients a list of the transactions that are set to close in the next 60 days, noted that the door is open for others to file, and ordered that any other successor trustees be disclosed by December 4, 2023. Ms. Duggan is to respond to the objections. The Court encouraged the parties to open the lines of communications and work through the small issues. The petition is continued to December 11, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: R. Cabrera. Matter not reported.

Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee And Establishing Procedure For The Appointment Of Future Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Sep-25-2023 Continued To Oct-25-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:

The Court hears extensive argument by the appearing objectors to the pregrant of the petition for order appointing successor trustee and establishing procedure for the appointment. Ms. Hung argues in support of the petition noting the urgent need for a successor trustee.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court stated that it could not go forward on the petition today since there are objections, ordered that written objections be on file by October 18, 2023, and continued the petition before the court today to October 25, 2023.

The petition for order appointing successor trustee, etc. is continued to Wednesday, October 25, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.
